rationale: Gal and McShane (2012) is cited in the theory section as evidence that closing debt accounts (small victories) can increase motivation to continue repaying debt. The citing paper then uses that prior result to position its own contribution, arguing that similar progress effects can be created within a single account through repayment-by-purchase. Because the cited work helps structure both the conceptual setup and the stated contribution, the citation plays a substantive role.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Gal and McShane's small-victories account of debt repayment motivation and extends it from progress across multiple debt accounts to progress cues within a single credit card bill via item/category repayment.
